source: R MASS::birthwt — Hosmer & Lemeshow 1989; 189 births at Baystate Medical Center Springfield MA 1986; outcome = low birthweight (<2500g)
notes: Control study: smoking consistently increases low-birthweight risk within each racial stratum. NonWhite mothers have higher baseline low-birthweight rates regardless of smoking, creating differential allocation, but the smoking effect direction is preserved. No reversal across strata.
